sources.title: Frequently asked questions on virtual currency transactions
sources.summary: IRS treats convertible virtual currency as property (Notice 2014-21); disposals are taxable; gain/loss on Form 8949 and Schedule D.
sources.reliability_notes: Primary tax-authority guidance.

sources.title: Instructions for Form 8949 (2025)
sources.summary: Reporting of capital asset dispositions including digital assets.

sources.title: 1040 (2025) Instructions — digital asset question
sources.summary: Filers must answer the digital-asset question whether or not a 1099-DA was received.

sources.title: Final regulations and related IRS guidance for reporting by brokers on sales and exchanges of digital assets
sources.summary: Brokers report gross proceeds for transactions on/after Jan 1 2025; basis for certain covered transactions on/after Jan 1 2026.

sources.title: IRS provides additional transition relief for brokers required to file information returns and backup withhold on certain digital asset sales
sources.summary: Transition relief on backup withholding for digital-asset broker reporting.

sources.title: IRS extends digital asset broker relief through 2027 under Notice 2025-33
sources.summary: Notice 2025-33 delays backup-withholding obligations to Jan 1 2027.
sources.reliability_notes: Tier-2 firm explainer of an IRS notice; used as secondary context alongside the IRS primary.

sources.title: Notice 2020-2: Virtual Currency Reporting on the FBAR
sources.summary: A foreign account holding only virtual currency is not currently FBAR-reportable; FinCEN intends to propose an amendment to include virtual currency.
sources.reliability_notes: Status as of 2026 review: amendment not finalized. Re-verify each quarter.

sources.title: Foreign Cryptocurrency: U.S. FBAR and FATCA Reporting Requirements
sources.summary: Explains FBAR/FATCA treatment of foreign crypto accounts, including mixed accounts.

sources.title: FinCEN guidance on convertible virtual currencies (money transmission)
sources.summary: Distinguishes hosted from unhosted wallets; non-transmitting unhosted-wallet software providers are not MSBs.
sources.reliability_notes: URL is the FinCEN guidance hub; confirm exact 2019 CVC guidance document at next review.

sources.title: Analysis of self-custody policy and the withdrawn unhosted-wallet proposal
sources.summary: Secondary context on self-custody policy debate; the 2020 unhosted-wallet proposal was withdrawn in 2024.
sources.reliability_notes: Tier-3 advocacy source; used only as secondary context, not as the sole support for the legality claim.

sources.title: Travel Rule Crypto in the United States (FinCEN)
sources.summary: Summary of US travel-rule implementation and the $3,000 threshold.
sources.reliability_notes: Tier-3; confirm threshold against the FinCEN funds-transfer/travel rule primary at next review.

sources.title: Instructions for Form 8854 (2025) — Expatriation
sources.summary: Covered-expatriate tests and §877A deemed-sale mechanics; thresholds for the relevant year.

sources.title: Expatriation Tax
sources.summary: Citizenship-based taxation and the exit-tax framework.

sources.title: 2026 Estate and Gift Tax Update
sources.summary: 2026 federal lifetime exemption $15M per individual; annual gift exclusion $19,000.
sources.reliability_notes: Tier-2; confirm figures against the IRS annual inflation-adjustment revenue procedure at next review.

sources.title: IRS Announces Increased Gift and Estate Tax Exemption Amounts for 2026
sources.summary: Corroborates the 2026 estate/gift exemption increase.

sources.title: Revenue Procedure 2025-32 (2026 inflation adjustments)
sources.summary: Primary IRS source for 2026 figures: $15,000,000 basic exclusion amount for decedents dying in 2026; $19,000 annual gift exclusion.
sources.reliability_notes: Tier-1 primary; supersedes the Tier-2 firm explainers for the estate/gift figures.

sources.title: IRS releases tax inflation adjustments for tax year 2026, including amendments from the One, Big, Beautiful Bill
sources.summary: IRS newsroom summary of Rev. Proc. 2025-32, confirming the 2026 estate and gift figures.

sources.title: FinCEN Guidance FIN-2019-G001 — Application of FinCEN Regulations to Certain Business Models Involving Convertible Virtual Currencies
sources.summary: Providers of unhosted wallets that do not accept and transmit value are not money transmitters; hosted wallet providers that receive, store, and transmit CVC are MSBs.
sources.reliability_notes: Tier-1 primary; the exact FIN-2019-G001 document. Supersedes the earlier generic guidance-hub reference for self-custody claims.

sources.title: Statement on the Approval of Spot Bitcoin Exchange-Traded Products (Release No. 34-99306, Jan 10 2024)
sources.summary: SEC approved listing and trading of spot bitcoin ETP shares on national exchanges (NYSE Arca, Nasdaq, Cboe BZX) on Jan 10, 2024.

sources.title: OECD Automatic Exchange of Information — CRS by jurisdiction
sources.summary: OECD's list of CRS-committed jurisdictions. The United States is absent; it operates FATCA instead of CRS.
sources.reliability_notes: Tier-1 framework source. Reviewer: confirm the live OECD CRS-by-jurisdiction URL resolves at next review; the US absence is well corroborated.

sources.title: DIAN Oficio 30470 de 2019 (crypto = intangible asset)
sources.summary: DIAN treats cryptoassets as intangible assets forming part of patrimonio; declared at year-end value.

sources.title: Ley 2277 de 2022 (Reforma Tributaria)
sources.summary: Permanent wealth tax (impuesto al patrimonio) on net worth >= 72,000 UVT; inheritances/donations taxed as ganancia ocasional at 15%.

sources.title: Banco de la República — régimen cambiario / crypto not channelable
sources.summary: Crypto is not foreign currency and cannot be channeled through the FX market; crypto-funded investment is not registrable.

sources.title: SFC — does not regulate, supervise or endorse crypto operations
sources.summary: SFC does not regulate or supervise crypto; supervised banks not authorized to intermediate crypto; no exchange licensing regime.

sources.title: DIAN — Declaración de Activos en el Exterior (Form 160)
sources.summary: Tax residents with foreign assets > 2,000 UVT must file the foreign-asset declaration; foreign-held Bitcoin counts.

sources.title: Colombia adopts CARF-aligned crypto reporting (DIAN Res. 000240/2025)
sources.summary: DIAN Resolución 000240/2025 requires crypto-asset service providers to report user/transaction data from TY2026 (CARF-aligned).
sources.reliability_notes: Tier-2 explainer of a DIAN resolution; confirm against the DIAN resolution text before publication.

sources.title: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries — Colombia individual residence
sources.summary: Residence triggered by >183 days in any rolling 365-day window; residents taxed on worldwide income and assets.

sources.title: Forced heirship (legítima) in Colombia
sources.summary: Forced heirship: legítima rigurosa 50% reserved for forced heirs; cuarta de mejoras 25%; libre disposición 25%.
sources.reliability_notes: Tier-2 notarial source summarizing the Código Civil; confirm against the Civil Code before publication.

sources.title: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries — Panama individual other taxes
sources.summary: No net-worth/wealth tax; no inheritance, estate, or gift tax.

sources.title: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries — Panama residence/territorial
sources.summary: Territorial taxation: only Panama-source income taxed; residence via >183 days or center of economic interest.

sources.title: Panama removed from the European Union AML list
sources.summary: Panama removed from the EU high-risk third-country AML list following FATF grey-list exit (Oct 2023).

sources.title: Panama Supreme Court rejects crypto law
sources.summary: Panama's 2022 crypto law was declared unconstitutional; no general crypto law is in force; crypto remains legal but unregulated.

sources.title: IBA International Estate Planning Guide — Panama
sources.summary: Private-interest foundations (Law 25/1995) hold assets outside forced-heirship/succession claims; canonical Panamanian succession vehicle.

sources.title: OECD — Commitments to the Crypto-Asset Reporting Framework
sources.summary: List of jurisdictions committed to first CARF exchanges by 2027. Panama and El Salvador are NOT among committed jurisdictions.

sources.title: Beneficial Owner Registry in Panama (Law 129 of 2020)
sources.summary: Law 129/2020 created a private (non-public) beneficial-owner register for Panama entities via resident agents.

sources.title: IMF Press Release 25/043 — El Salvador 40-month EFF approved
sources.summary: IMF approved a 40-month EFF; program made Bitcoin acceptance voluntary and limits public-sector Bitcoin activity.

sources.title: El Salvador reforms Bitcoin Law to end mandatory use (29 Jan 2025)
sources.summary: Jan 29 2025 amendment removed Bitcoin's legal-tender/mandatory-acceptance status; acceptance now voluntary; taxes no longer payable in BTC.
sources.reliability_notes: Tier-2 media; corroborated by IMF program documents. Confirm against the Diario Oficial amendment text before publication.

sources.title: El Salvador ICT — Digital Assets Law (Trade.gov)
sources.summary: LEAD (Digital Assets Issuance Law, 2023) regulates issuers/providers; CNAD (Comisión Nacional de Activos Digitales) licenses and supervises.

sources.title: IBA International Estate Planning Guide — El Salvador
sources.summary: Civil-law forced heirship (legítima) reserves a portion for forced heirs; notarial/registry succession.

sources.title: ATO — How to work out and report CGT on crypto
sources.summary: Bitcoin is a CGT asset/property; disposal is a CGT event; 50% CGT discount if held >=12 months by individuals.

sources.title: ATO — Residency tests (TR 2023/1)
sources.summary: Four residency tests: resides, domicile, 183-day, Commonwealth superannuation.

sources.title: ATO — Crypto asset as a personal use asset
sources.summary: Personal-use-asset gain disregarded only if acquisition cost <= AUD 10,000 and asset mainly for personal use; investment holdings do not qualify.

sources.title: ATO — SMSF investing in crypto assets
sources.summary: Bitcoin may be held in an SMSF if the deed/strategy permit and super rules met (sole-purpose test, separation of assets, fund-name wallet, annual audit; cannot acquire from related party).

sources.title: AUSTRAC — Digital currency exchange providers
sources.summary: Digital-currency exchanges must register with AUSTRAC; being rebranded to VASPs per FATF terminology.

sources.title: Parliament of Australia — Corporations Amendment (Digital Assets Framework) Bill 2025 digest
sources.summary: Digital Assets Framework: creates Digital Asset Platform and Tokenised Custody Platform products under the AFSL/ASIC regime; Royal Assent Apr 2026; substantive obligations commence Apr 2027.

sources.title: ATO — OECD CARF and domestic crypto reporting
sources.summary: Australia committed to CARF (2023); confirmed implementation Dec 2025; first crypto data exchange expected ~2028. CRS participant since 2015.

sources.title: Council of Financial Regulators — Potential Policy Responses to De-banking in Australia
sources.summary: Documents crypto-business de-banking as a recognised problem and policy responses.

sources.title: ATO — How changing residency affects CGT (CGT event I1)
sources.summary: Ceasing Australian tax residency triggers CGT event I1 (s104-160 ITAA 1997): a deemed disposal at market value of assets that are not taxable Australian property, including cryptocurrency. A taxpayer may instead elect to keep the assets in the Australian CGT net. The 50% discount can apply; marginal rates up to ~47% incl. Medicare levy.
sources.reliability_notes: Tier-1 ATO. Election and per-case mechanics need an adviser.

sources.title: Panama Qualified Investor Visa — investment thresholds (2026)
sources.summary: Qualified Investor Visa grants permanent residency; real-estate threshold US$300,000 until 15 Oct 2026, reverting to US$500,000 after; securities US$500,000; fixed-deposit US$750,000. Corroborated across multiple immigration-advisory sources.
sources.reliability_notes: Tier-3 immigration-advisory; the threshold/date is an immigration-program fact corroborated by many sources, not a legal/tax conclusion. Confirm against the live Panama immigration decree at application date.

sources.title: FTA (ESTV) — Cryptocurrencies and taxation
sources.summary: Bitcoin is a taxable asset for wealth tax (valued at FTA year-end rate); private-investor capital gains on movable assets are income-tax exempt (DBG art.16(3)).

sources.title: OECD — Switzerland tax-residency rules
sources.summary: Unlimited tax liability via domicile, >=30 days' presence with gainful activity, or >=90 days without.

sources.title: ch.ch — Inheritance tax (cantonal)
sources.summary: No federal inheritance or gift tax; levied by cantons; spouses/registered partners exempt in all cantons; descendants exempt in most but not all cantons.

sources.title: FINMA — first DLT trading facility licensed (Mar 2025)
sources.summary: DLT Act (in force Aug 2021) created ledger-based securities and the FINMA DLT trading-facility license; first license granted Mar 2025.

sources.title: SIF — Automatic Exchange of Information (CRS)
sources.summary: Switzerland participates in CRS (in force 2017; first exchange 2018; 100+ partners).

sources.title: Switzerland delays crypto-asset information exchange to 2027
sources.summary: Switzerland approved CARF adoption; crypto information exchange postponed to 2027 at the earliest.

sources.title: Bär & Karrer — Revised Swiss inheritance law (in force 1 Jan 2023)
sources.summary: 2023 reform reduced reserved portions: descendants from 3/4 to 1/2 of intestate share; parents' compulsory portion abolished; spouse 1/2 unchanged.
sources.reliability_notes: Tier-2 firm briefing on the Civil Code reform; confirm against ZGB text before client use.

sources.title: admin.ch — Switzerland ratifies the Hague Trust Convention
sources.summary: Hague Trust Convention in force in Switzerland from 1 July 2007; foreign trusts recognized, but overridable under art.15 by mandatory law incl. forced heirship.

sources.title: Federal Department of Finance — Lump-sum taxation
sources.summary: Lump-sum (expenditure-based) taxation available to qualifying foreigners without Swiss gainful activity; a cantonal option (abolished in ~5 cantons).

sources.title: Ley 1934 de 2018 (reforma del régimen sucesoral / libertad testamentaria)
sources.summary: Amended Civil Code art. 1242: merged the cuarta de mejoras and cuarta de libre disposición into a single freely-disposable quarter-mass, so post-2018 a testator with forced heirs reserves the legítima (half) and may freely dispose of the other half.

sources.title: Código Civil Colombiano — De las Asignaciones Forzosas (Libro 3, Título 5)
sources.summary: Asignaciones forzosas: legítima rigurosa = half the estate divided among legitimaries per intestate rules; plus alimentos and porción conyugal.
sources.reliability_notes: Tier-2 host reproducing the Código Civil text; the operative 2018 change is in src-co-ley1934 (Tier-1).

sources.title: Snapshot: succession law in Panama
sources.summary: Testators in Panama have freedom of disposition (no reserved portion); intestate succession gives children equal shares, per stirpes, with the spouse. Corroborates IBA guide.

sources.title: Código Civil de El Salvador (OAS-hosted text)
sources.publisher: Organization of American States (hosting the El Salvador Civil Code)
sources.summary: Bello-derived Civil Code. Intestate succession calls children, parents, and spouse by order, dividing equally within each order; spouse loses the intestate share on unjustified abandonment. (Testate legítima fraction not extracted this pass.)
sources.reliability_notes: Tier-1 code text; the testate forced-heirship/legítima fraction was not located in this pass and remains an open question.

sources.title: Swiss Gift & Inheritance Tax — cantonal treatment of descendants
sources.summary: Spouses/registered partners exempt in all cantons; direct descendants exempt in virtually all cantons except Appenzell Innerrhoden (CHF 300k allowance/child then ~1%), Vaud (allowances then progressive), and Neuchâtel (nominal 3%, allowances reduce to ~0).

sources.title: Family Provision Claims — NSW Succession Act 2006 (s57/s60) and per-state Acts
sources.publisher: Turnbull Hill Lawyers (summarizing the Succession Act 2006 (NSW))
sources.summary: Eligible persons (spouse, former spouse, de facto, child, dependent grandchild/household member, close personal relationship) may seek provision from the estate; NSW s57 eligibility, s60 sixteen factors. Other states: Succession Act 1981 (QLD), Administration and Probate Act 1958 (VIC), Inheritance (Family Provision) Act 1972 (SA).
sources.reliability_notes: Tier-2 summary of Tier-1 statutes; statutes named for confirmation.

sources.title: Colombia — sociedad conyugal (default marital regime)
sources.summary: Absent a prenuptial agreement (capitulaciones), marriage creates a sociedad conyugal (community of marital gains); property acquired during the marriage is shared on dissolution.

sources.title: Switzerland — marriage economic consequences (default regime)
sources.summary: Default matrimonial regime is participation in acquisitions (Errungenschaftsbeteiligung): separate and acquired property managed separately; on dissolution, acquired-property surplus is shared.

sources.title: Australia — division of matrimonial property (Family Law Act 1975)
sources.summary: No community-property regime; the Family Law Act 1975 governs property settlement on separation by reference to contributions (financial and non-financial) and future needs, not a fixed share.
sources.reliability_notes: AIFS (govt research institute) summarizing the Family Law Act 1975 (Tier-1 statute named).

sources.title: El Salvador — testamentary freedom debate (secondary analyses)
sources.publisher: Secondary legal analyses (incl. Univ. de El Salvador study materials)
sources.summary: Several secondary sources state El Salvador follows ABSOLUTE testamentary freedom (no forced-heirship legítima), conflicting with forced-heirship framings elsewhere. Used only to flag the conflict, not to assert a conclusion.
sources.reliability_notes: Tier-3; conflicting with other secondary sources. Per the conflict rule the testate-reserve question stays unresolved pending the Código Civil text.
